science.title: Quadratic gravity theory reshapes quantum view of Big Bang
science.description: Waterloo scientists have developed a new way to understand how the universe began, and it could change what we know about the Big Bang and the earliest moments of cosmic history. Their work suggests that the universe's rapid early expansion could have arisen naturally from a deeper, more complete theory of quantum gravity. The paper, "Ultraviolet completion of the Big Bang in quadratic gravity," appears in Physical Review Letters.

science.title: Black hole mergers test the limits of general relativity
science.description: General relativity stands as one of the bedrock theories in modern physics. Its strange view of relative time and space has been confirmed by countless experimental and observational tests, from rotational frame dragging to the radiation of gravitational waves. But there is reason to believe that it is not the final say on the nature of space and time.

science.title: Watch: Antennae Galaxies engaged in a cosmic duel

ai.title: Rethinking brain-like artificial intelligence: New study reveals hidden mismatches
ai.description: A new study by York University researchers has found a potential striking flaw in artificial intelligence (AI) models. Artificial neural networks (ANNs), a type of AI model built to solve vision tasks for computers, have surprisingly emerged as the current best understanding of how our own brain's visual system works, in the last decade. But does current AI really work like a primate brain?
